title: Exceptions should not be thrown in finally blocks
description: Exceptions should not be thrown in finally blocksAnalyze your codeintentionality-completemaintainabilityCode Smellerror-handlingsuspiciousWhy is this an issue?More InfoIf an exception is already being thrown within thetryblock or caught in acatchblock, throwing another exception in thefinallyblock will override the original exception. This means that the original exception’s message and stack trace will be lost, potentially making it challenging to diagnose and troubleshoot the root cause of the problem.try { // Some work which end up throwing an exception throw new ArgumentException(); } finally { // Cleanup throw new InvalidOperationException(); // Noncompliant: will mask the ArgumentException }try { // Some work which end up throwing an exception throw new ArgumentException(); } finally
recommendation: Review and fix according to the rule guidelines.

checks.message: Exceptions thrown in finally blocks mask exceptions from try/catch blocks

logic.reasoning: Detects throw statements within finally blocks. Throwing exceptions from finally blocks masks any exceptions from the try or catch blocks, losing critical debugging information. The pattern matches 'finally { ... throw new' sequences within method bodies.

should_trigger: try { DoWork(); } finally { throw new InvalidOperationException(); }
try { Process(); } finally { Cleanup(); throw new Exception(); }

logic.self_critique_notes: Pattern detects 'finally { ... throw' sequences. Edge case: Try-catch within finally block that throws is technically allowed but may need separate handling. Pattern uses non-greedy match to avoid cross-block matching.
